title: senecio squalidus subsp. siculus
intro: Senecio squalidus subsp. siculus, also known as Jacob's ladder, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, Jacob's ladder has been used for its medicinal properties, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone content. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as supporting respiratory health, reducing inflammation, and acting as an antioxidant. Jacob's ladder can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
